title: ‘Anti-woke' fallout? BlackRock, Fidelity and other asset managers soften climate-change efforts, report says
text: The world's largest asset managers remain far off track to meet their commitments to claim net-zero carbon emissions by 2050, according to a global study released by FinanceMap during European hours Tuesday.

title: State Street (STT) Rides on Expansion Efforts Amid Expense Woes
text: Higher rates, inorganic growth strategy and global reach are likely to keep supporting State Street (STT). However, rising expenses and substantial exposure to fee income are major near-term woes.

title: State Street to Service abrdn's First Actively Managed ETF In Europe
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) today announced that it has been appointed by abrdn to service its first actively managed exchange traded fund (ETF) in Europe. State Street will provide abrdn, a leading European asset manager with £500bn1 in assets under management and administration (AUMA), with custody, fund accounting, order-taking, transfer agency services and ETF servicing. State Street has been working closely with abrdn to support the firm's expansion into t.

title: Term of the Week: Net Interest Income
text: Higher interest rates boosted big banks' net interest income in the second quarter, which is the profit banks earn from charging higher interest rates to borrowers than they pay out to depositors and account holders.

title: State Street Corporation Declares Third-Quarter Dividend on its Common Stock, a 10% per Share Increase Over Prior Quarter
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) today announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.69 per share of common stock, payable on October 12, 2023 to common shareholders of record at the close of business on October 2, 2023. The dividend represents a 10% per share increase from the second quarter 2023 dividend of $0.63 per share. title: State Street Corporation Declares Dividends on Its Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock Series “D”, “F” and “G”
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE:STT) today announced a cash dividend on each of the below outstanding series of non-cumulative perpetual preferred stock: Series D (represented by depositary shares, each representing a 1/4000th interest in a share of Series D preferred stock). The cash dividend is in the amount of $1,475.00 per share of Series D preferred stock (resulting in a distribution of approximately $0.3687 per depositary share) and is payable on September 15, 2023.

title: State Street downgraded on pricing pressure déjà vu
text: Analysts at UBS have downgraded their rating on State Street Corporation (NYSE:STT) from ‘Buy' to ‘Neutral' following the bank's 2Q earnings report as it once again faces pricing pressure. The analysts also lowered their 12-month price target on the stock from $90 to $72 based on a roughly nine times multiple of their 2024 earnings per share estimate.

title: State Street beats earnings estimates but faces sell-off as revenue falls short
text: State Street Corporation (NYSE:STT)delivered 2Q earnings that came in ahead of estimates, but its stock was punished on Friday as fickle traders digested its results. The US financial institution posted 2Q earnings of $2.17 a share, beating estimates of around $2.08, but topline revenue missed the mark at $3.11 billion.

title: State Street Corporation (STT) Q2 Earnings Beat Estimates
text: State Street Corporation (STT) came out with quarterly earnings of $2.17 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.08 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.94 per share a year ago.

title: State Street stock drops after net interest income and revenue fall short
text: State Street Corp. STT, -4.52% stock was down by 4.5% after it reported a better-than-expected second-quarter profit but fell short of its revenue and net interest income marks. State Street said its earnings for the three months ended June 30 rose 2% to $763 million, or $2.17 a share, from $747 million, or $1.91 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: State Street beats profit estimates as interest income rises
text: State Street Corp on Friday beat profit estimates for the second quarter as the bank's interest income benefited from the U.S. Federal Reserve's policy tightening and assets under management rose amid a recovery in the equity markets.

title: State Street Continues Latin America Growth; Announces Global Custody Mandate with Confuturo
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE:STT) today announced that it has been appointed to provide custody and fund order services for Confuturo, a Chile-based insurance company that offers flexible, health, temporary life, travel insurance, and pensioned consumer credit solutions. The company is one of the largest provider of annuities in Chile. title: State Street (STT) Expands in Latin America, Forays in Colombia
text: State Street's (STT) debut in Colombia underlines its efforts to expand in the Latin American region. The company will benefit from the dynamic and fast-growing market.

title: State Street to Provide Data-Driven Geopolitical Risk Insights to Clients
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) announced today that it has entered into an arrangement with GeoQuant, a Fitch Solutions Company, to make available geopolitical and country risk indicators produced and calculated by GeoQuant in State Street's own macro strategy research. The GeoQuant indicators measure and predict political risk daily and systematically with a fusion of PhD-level political and computer science. The full suite of GeoQuant's indicators are available.

title: State Street Appoints Jessica Donohue to Head of Global Investment Insights, Sustainability and Impact
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) announced today that it has appointed Executive Vice President Jessica Donohue as head of Global Investment Insights, Sustainability and Impact. Donohue succeeds Rick Lacaille who announced his retirement earlier this year. She will report to Lou Maiuri, president, chief operating officer, and head of Investment Services. title: State Street Enters Colombian Market to Support Continued Growth in Latin America
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) announced today its expansion into Colombia, scaling its servicing capabilities for institutional clients in Latin America. Andres Zambrano Burgl will manage the Colombia office and lead sales in the country. Zambrano is based in Bogota and will report to Maria Ximena Vasquez, State Street's head of Sales for Latin America and The Caribbean (ex Brazil). title: 4 Analyst Favorite Blue Chip Companies Are Expected To Raise Their Dividends This Week
text: After years of a low interest rate environment, which has been trending higher over the last year, many investors have turned to equities not only for the growth potential but also for solid and dependable dividends that help to provide an income stream.

title: State Street: Good, But Not Good Enough
text: State Street Corporation, a leading global provider of financial services, has consistent dividend growth and a yield of 3.4%, but its total return in the past decade has been disappointing. The company has transformed from a back-office service provider to an enterprise outsourcer, focusing on cost reduction, data management, and technology integration, which provides it with a competitive edge. Despite ongoing macroeconomic challenges, State Street remains focused on expanding capabilities and investing in growth initiatives, but its slow streamlining process may not offer strong long-term total returns for dividend investors.

title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For July 2023
text: Despite rising interest rates and stubborn inflation, equities have shown resilience in 2023 with the SPDR S&P Trust ETF adding 6.48% in June and up 16.80% year-to-date. My top 10 list of high dividend yield stocks has generated an annualized rate of return of 13.19% since its inception in November 2020. The list from June posted a solid gain of 5.63%, beating VYM but not SPY.
